title: phoradendron leucarpum subsp. tomentosum
intro: Phoradendron leucarpum subsp. tomentosum is a subspecies of the American mistletoe plant, a hemiparasitic plant that grows on various tree species in North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of P. leucarpum subsp. tomentosum include reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
